title: Array Functions: Merging, Slicing, and More
description: Combine arrays, extract slices, and check existence.

slides.title: Useful Array Functions
slides.content:
# Common Operations

*   `count()` – returns number of elements
*   `array_merge()` – merges two or more arrays
*   `array_slice()` – extracts a portion of an array
*   `array_splice()` – removes and replaces portion
*   `in_array()` – checks if a value exists
*   `array_key_exists()` – checks if a key exists
*   `array_keys()` – returns all keys
*   `array_values()` – returns all values

slides.title: Slice and Splice
slides.content:
```php
<?php
$colors = ["red", "green", "blue", "yellow", "purple"];

$slice = array_slice($colors, 1, 2); // start at index 1, length 2
print_r($slice); // ["green", "blue"]

// array_splice modifies original, can remove and insert
$removed = array_splice($colors, 2, 1, ["pink", "orange"]);
print_r($removed); // ["blue"]
print_r($colors); // ["red", "green", "pink", "orange", "yellow", "purple"]
?>
```

slides.title: Checking Existence
slides.codeSnippet:
<?php
$fruits = ["apple", "banana", "cherry"];
if (in_array("banana", $fruits)) {
    echo "Banana found!\n";
}
$person = ["name" => "Alice", "age" => 25];
if (array_key_exists("age", $person)) {
    echo "Age key exists\n";
}
$keys = array_keys($person);
print_r($keys);
$values = array_values($person);
print_r($values);
?>

quizData.question: Which function returns the number of elements in an array?
quizData.explanation: Both `count()` and `sizeof()` are aliases and return the number of elements. The most common is `count()`.
